summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 9 warning, 12 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `.team/features/cron-based-outer-loop/tasks/*/artifacts/` — No `test-output.txt` stored in any task's artifact directory; claimed test counts (553 tests) cannot be independently verified from the artifact trail alone — store test runner output as a task artifact in future runs.
The prior 🟡 findings from earlier runs (args leakage, Unicode sanitization gap, stale-item stranding) are all confirmed closed in run_3. Eval written to `tasks/task-4/eval.md`.

🟡 `cron.mjs:86` — `listProjectItems` called bare inside the outer `try` with no inner catch; if the GitHub API throws (network failure, auth expiry), the error propagates as an unhandled rejection with no `console.error` log — the `finally` block still releases the lock, but the failure is invisible in the cron log; add a test where `listProjectItems` throws and assert `console.error` is invoked with a useful message

🟡 `test/cron-tick.test.mjs:399` — Stale+ready coexistence: dispatch priority not asserted; after stale recovery, item #3 (mutated to `"ready"`) appears first in `readyItems` and gets dispatched ahead of item #4 (originally `"Ready"`), but the test only asserts `runCalled === true` — a regression that changes dispatch ordering would pass silently; add an assertion on which `issueNumber` was dispatched
- Prior 🟡 findings from runs 1–2 (commentIssue guard, Unicode sanitization, args leakage, lock-release spies, console-restore in try/finally) confirmed closed
- No critical findings — two new 🟡 backlog items added
Two prior 🟡 security findings are **closed** in run_3:
No 🔴 critical findings. Two 🟡 warnings for the backlog:

🟡 `bin/lib/cron.mjs:101` — `staleItem.status = "ready"` mutates the input array element to populate the ready pool via a filter 5 lines later; coupling is non-obvious — collect recovered items into an explicit variable and merge with `readyItems`

🟡 `bin/lib/cron.mjs:20` — `readProjectNumber` duplicates `outer-loop.mjs:117` body-for-body (same regex, same try/catch, same contract); extract to shared utility to avoid split maintenance (carry-forward)
